title: Arrays - values in a row, reachable at once
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: An array keeps items in order, side by side, so any one can be reached instantly by its position (index).
explanation: An array is the simplest container: a block of slots in a row, numbered from 0. Because the slots sit together in memory, the computer can jump straight to slot 5 without walking past 0 to 4 - reading by index costs the same whether the array holds ten things or ten million. The trade-off comes later: growing it or inserting in the middle is the expensive part.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: Indexing starts at 0, so a[0] is 10.

exercises.question: Reading an item by its index, a[i], takes...
options: the same time no matter how big the array is
hints: The slots sit together, so the computer jumps straight there.
It doesn't walk through the earlier items.
Constant time - the same, whatever the size.

exercises.question:
Arrays number their positions starting from which number?
